edges.desc: The magic items are included in the adventure as described in the text.
Dragon creatures inhabit the cursed shipwreck as part of the adventure scenario.
The dungeon master provides quests to player characters during gameplay.
Sailors stay at Dragon's Rest as their home base during the adventure.
The island has been a battlefield in the ongoing conflict involving dragons.
Dragon's Rest is located within Stormwreck Isle.
Various creatures reside on the island, contributing to its status as a battleground.
The action of starting the game involves using the character sheet item to prepare characters.
Players choose characters including Cleric, indicating alignment with the Cleric group.
Characters chosen as Clerics are members of the Cleric group.

The crypts are located within the region of Delzoun.
The alleyways are located within the city of Neverwinter.
Clerics in the service of gods defend against threats.
The ruins are located within the former territory of the Netherese Empire.
Roads are part of the Forgotten Realms.
Deadly monsters attack adventurers traveling through the Realms.
Stormwreck Isle is situated within the Sword Coast region.
Tales inspire adventurers to pursue distant lands and challenges.
The main character is part of the adventure party group, which requires at least four characters to function properly.
The sidekick is part of the adventure party group, functioning as an auxiliary character managed by a player alongside a main character when needed.
The adventure party participates in the Dragon's of Stormwreck Isle, which is the setting for the campaign requiring adjustments in player count.
Bahamut created the metallic dragons during the world's creation.
Tiamat created the chromatic dragons during the world's creation.
The three metallic dragons attacked Sharruth to imprison her beneath the ocean floor.
Sharruth is entombed beneath the ocean floor.
The powerful magic from the ancient dragon is stored within Stormwreck Isle.
Runara killed the blue dragon wyrmling who attempted to harness the magic at Stormwreck Isle.
The seagrow caves are a subsection of the broader region known as Stormwreck Isle.
The cursed shipwreck lies on the island of Stormwreck Isle.
The clifftop observatory is situated on the island of Stormwreck Isle.
The magical hot spring is located on the island of Stormwreck Isle.
Guardians are present at the hot spring, safeguarding its mystical properties.
The bones of the gold dragon are stored within the hold of the cursed shipwreck.
A blue dragon's grandson occupies a lair within the clifftop observatory.
A bronze wyrmling is imprisoned within the clifftop observatory’s lair.
The Stormwreck Isle location is marked on the map, implying it is part of the map's depicted area.
Secret doors, mentioned as elements shown on maps, are part of the Stormwreck Isle location.
The player version map is connected to the original map as it depicts the same area but excludes secret details.
The Dungeon Master presents challenges to the characters as part of the game experience.
The Dungeon Master introduces encounters that the characters must navigate during play.
The Dungeon Master describes events in response to the characters' actions, tying into the concept of responses.
The Dungeon Master rolls the d20 die for determining outcomes, particularly for attacks.
The Dungeon Master assumes the role of monsters and NPCs, effectively controlling their behaviors and actions.
The text indicates that the Dungeon Master is not competing against player characters, suggesting an Allied With relationship to maintain collaborative storytelling.
The text states that the DM "runs monsters," implying authority over creatures, establishing a Commands relationship.

The text advises modifying encounters to suit preferences, indicating a Modification relationship between the Dungeon Master and encounters. Note: While "modifies" is not explicitly listed as a relationship type, the closest actionable intent aligns with the DM’s role in adjusting encounters.

Though not explicitly stated, the text mentions keeping a notepad handy at the table, indirectly associating the bed (an item) with the table (another item) as potential storage locations. This remains speculative, however, due to indirect inference.
The text notes tracking initiative order, which implicitly ties to the "track" location, forming a Tracks relationship despite ambiguity in the exact nature of interaction.
The Dungeon Master's most important task is figuring out how much to tell the players and when.
The Dungeon Master provides information to players, ensuring they receive everything necessary to make decisions within gameplay constraints.
Making Intelligence (Investigation) checks grants players knowledge about their surroundings.
Performing Wisdom (Perception) checks allows players to gain additional knowledge about their environment.
As characters perform checks (e.g., Investigation or Perception), the Dungeon Master reveals progressively more detailed information about discoveries.
The *flaming_sphere* attack (an action) is directed toward the *fire_snake_egg*, illustrating a hostile interaction between the spell and the item.
The *adventure_text* item contains textual cues that reference the *stat_block* item, guiding readers to appendices containing detailed creature statistics.

The fissure in the cave wall ([se]) is positioned near the southeastern tip of the island, implied by contextual proximity in the narrative.
The cursed shipwreck is depicted as part of the maritime dangers encountered in the sea, suggesting coexistence within the same region.

The Dungeon Master assumes authority over the players (second-level characters) by guiding the narrative and handling game mechanics.
The Dungeon Master implicitly assigns scenarios or challenges to the players as part of the demonstration, aligning with the phrase "running you through a quick demo on how to play D&D."
The players (heroes) inhabit the world around them as depicted by the Dungeon Master.

The Dungeon Master uses the d20 die to resolve actions, such as determining outcomes for the monster. Note: While "rolling" is not explicitly mapped to a predefined action type, the association between the DM and the d20 die is implied by the text.
The Dungeon Master ensures the safety and coherence of the environment described as "the world around you."
The text describes leaving Neverwinter heading for Stormwreck Isle, establishing a directional path between these two locations.

Although not explicitly stated, the journey from Neverwinter to Stormwreck Isle implies navigation across the sea, indirectly linking the vessel/item to the sea. However, due to ambiguity, this relation remains tentative.
While not explicitly mentioned, the narrative assumes the player’s group (likely second-level characters) originates from Neverwinter, creating a contextual inhabitant relationship.
Dark clouds threatening a violent storm implicitly associate the overarching *threat* event with environmental danger, though the storm itself is not a listed entity.
Lightning flashes across the sky (referred to as [atmosphere]), establishing a spatial adjacency between the lightning phenomenon and the atmospheric environment.
A monster (specifically [merrow_extortionist]) hauls itself up onto the deck (sea_deck), indicating physical contact or adjacency between the creature and the deck location.
The Scaled Queen claims sovereignty over the waters, establishing her authority over the location.
The act of collecting tribute begins an interaction initiated by the speaker toward the Scaled Queen.
The text indicates that engaging in combat involves fighting the merrow extortionist, establishing a direct adversarial relationship where Combat targets this monster.
The narrative presents the situation and leaves the outcome to the players' choices, implying that the "Start" action initiates engagement scenarios like Combat.

The text describes rolling a 20-sided die as part of resolving actions during combat, indicating the dice is used in the Combat event. Note: While "Action_uses" is inferred from implied functionality, it aligns with the described mechanic of requiring dice rolls for resolution.
The Scaled Queen carries the Blessing item, which is stored within her possession.
The Scaled Queen is described as receiving a blessing from Demogorgon, the Prince of Demons, establishing an alliance with the Demons group.

The Hold location contains the Pouch of Gold Pieces item as part of the goods referenced in the text.

A Charisma (Intimidation) check is used to convince the merrow extortionist of impending crew attacks.

While the drowned sailors resemble sailors in appearance, the text emphasizes they are distinct (gray-skinned, drowned), but their association with the sailor group is implicit through their attire.

Encounters with zombies (linked to danger events) require players to make intelligence checks to uncover combat tactics.

Tarak formerly served the Gilded Gallows, a thieves' guild, as a ruthless poisoner before fleeing due to guilt over killing his lover.

Tarak uses heart cap mushrooms harvested from the sea caves to craft potions of healing.
Tarak was affiliated with a thieves' guild, though he abandoned it after causing harm to his lover.
Investigating Tarak’s tattoos reveals historical associations with the Gilded Gallows, triggering curiosity about his past.

Varnoth witnessed a ship altering its course and crashing into rocks, linking her to the vessel's fate.
